title: shared_preferences
objectives: Know its limits vs Hive/secure storage

sections.markdown: `shared_preferences` persists small primitive values (bool, int, double, String, List<String>) using the platform's native key-value store: SharedPreferences on Android, NSUserDefaults on iOS.
Perfect for tiny app settings (theme, onboarded flag, last-used tab). Not encrypted, not fast for big data, and easy to inspect on a rooted device — never store tokens here.
Storing JSON blobs, objects, or auth tokens. For structured data → Hive/Drift/Isar. For secrets → flutter_secure_storage. shared_preferences is for tiny flags and settings.
`getInstance()` is async — the modern approach uses `SharedPreferencesAsync` (newer API) or the sync-after-init pattern. Migrate to the new SharedPreferencesAsync/WithCache APIs when convenient.

flashcards.back: bool, int, double, String, List<String>. That's it.

quiz.explain: Serialize to a small string; better yet, use Hive.

challenges.text: Persist a list of recent search strings.
Migrate from shared_preferences to Hive without losing state.
Adopt SharedPreferencesAsync/WithCache in a legacy codebase.

interviewQuestions: When is shared_preferences the right tool?
